title: AI — Consumption & Cost
description: Token throughput and spend — the FinOps view. Token counters are live. **Cost is still not meaningful**: computedCost requires configured cost rates, and LibreChat's Agents API returns an all-zero usage object for headless API-key calls (#38), so usage is frequently null at the source. LiteLLM (#102) is what makes it authoritative.

panels.title: What is and is not trustworthy here

options.content:
### What is and is not trustworthy here

**Gateway series (litellm_gateway_*): live and authoritative.** The litellm-exporter bridge reads LiteLLM's spend log — every call LibreChat makes through the gateway is metered there per model and virtual key (#102), including the ones LibreChat itself reports as zero usage.

**App-side tokens (nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total): empty until #38 is fixed upstream.** LibreChat's Agents API returns an all-zero usage object for headless API-key calls, and the app deliberately records "unmetered" rather than a confidently wrong zero.

**Cost: needs cost rates configured per agent** (computeCost returns null otherwise), and local Ollama models are genuinely $0 — flat zero spend with traffic is correct until a hosted model runs through the gateway.

panels.title: Tokens/sec by type
panels.description: App-side series (nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total) stay empty while LibreChat reports zero usage on headless calls (#38); the gateway series (litellm_gateway_tokens_total, via the litellm-exporter bridge) are the authoritative meter from LiteLLM's spend log (#102).

panels.title: Tokens/sec by model
panels.description: Gateway series come from LiteLLM's spend log via litellm-exporter — authoritative per-model counts (#102). App-side series await LibreChat usage reporting (#38).

panels.title: Spend — requires cost rates + #102
panels.description: Fed by nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total / run_cost_total, which increment only when LibreChat's Agents API reports usage — and it currently returns all-zero usage for headless API-key calls (issue #38, verified live), so these stay empty even with the metrics code deployed. The authoritative per-model token/spend data lives in the LiteLLM gateway (#102): its admin UI and /spend/logs meter every call.

panels.title: Spend (24h)
panels.description: Fed by nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total / run_cost_total, which increment only when LibreChat's Agents API reports usage — and it currently returns all-zero usage for headless API-key calls (issue #38, verified live), so these stay empty even with the metrics code deployed. The authoritative per-model token/spend data lives in the LiteLLM gateway (#102): its admin UI and /spend/logs meter every call.

panels.title: Spend rate by model
panels.description: Gateway series show a flat $0 for local Ollama models — they are genuinely free; real spend appears when a hosted model (claude-sonnet) runs through the gateway. App-side series additionally need cost rates configured and LibreChat usage reporting (#38).

panels.title: Cost per successful run
panels.description: Gateway spend over app-side successful runs. $0 with traffic is correct while only free local models run; hosted-model spend appears here per run once configured.

panels.title: Gateway budgets — requires #102 (LiteLLM)
panels.description: LiteLLM's Prometheus /metrics endpoint is an enterprise feature — on the OSS proxy this panel stays empty (404 from /metrics, verified). Authoritative spend lives in LiteLLM's own Postgres and /spend/logs API, and per-model token counts come from nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total once the app-side metrics (PR #110) are deployed.

panels.title: LiteLLM spend by key/model
panels.description: From the litellm-exporter bridge over LiteLLM's /spend/logs — works on the OSS proxy (its own Prometheus /metrics is enterprise-only). Local Ollama models cost $0, so a flat 0 with traffic is correct; hosted models (claude-sonnet) will show real spend.

panels.title: Tokens per run (avg, by model)
panels.description: Fed by nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total / run_cost_total, which increment only when LibreChat's Agents API reports usage — and it currently returns all-zero usage for headless API-key calls (issue #38, verified live), so these stay empty even with the metrics code deployed. The authoritative per-model token/spend data lives in the LiteLLM gateway (#102): its admin UI and /spend/logs meter every call.
